column2: of / from — una tazza di caffè
on / about — un libro su Roma
that / which / who — la persona che conosco
to do / make — faccio / fai / fa
can / be able — posso / puoi / può
must / have to — devo / devi / deve
to say / tell — dico / dici / dice
to stay / be — sto / stai / sta
to go out — esco / esci / esce
The apple is red. (adjective follows noun + agrees)
I give him the apple. (indirect pronoun before the verb)
I give it to him. (gli + la → gliela, stacked before verb)
I have to give it to him. (modal + infinitive; pronouns attach)
I can't eat it. (non before verb; pronoun on infinitive)
I gave her the apple. (passato prossimo + pronoun before ho)
When are you giving it to me? (mi → me before another pronoun)
Could I have the bill? (polite: potrei)
There's no one. (double negative is correct)
How are you? / I'm well. (stare for state & health)
I like coffee. (piacere = 'is pleasing to me'; one thing → piace)
Do you like it? (swap person with indirect pronoun)
I like to eat. (a verb counts as singular → piace)
